commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `skills/gog/SKILL.md` so the bundled gog skill's brew installer uses `gogcli` instead of `steipete/tap/gogcli`.

PR surface: Docs 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main passes the `skills/gog/SKILL.md` formula through the parser and installer into `brew install steipete/tap/gogcli`, and live checks show that tap formula now returns 404 while `gogcli` resolves in homebrew-core.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the existing PR is already the focused candidate fix; the remaining action is normal maintainer merge and check handling.

target.title: fix(skills): point gog brew install at homebrew-core gogcli (#95017)
target.body:
## Summary

Onboarding's skill-dependency installer fails to install the `gog` skill because its bundled metadata points at a Homebrew formula that no longer exists in that tap:

```json
"formula": "steipete/tap/gogcli"
```

`gogcli` has since graduated to **homebrew-core**, and the formula was removed from `steipete/tap`. The installer builds `brew install steipete/tap/gogcli`, which now fails with:

```text
Warning: Skipping steipete/tap because it is not trusted. Run `brew trust steipete/tap` to trust it.
Warning: No available formula or cask with the name "steipete/tap/gogcli".
```

This PR points the `gog` skill at the canonical core formula `gogcli`, so the install command becomes `brew install gogcli`. Using the core formula (vs. `openclaw/tap/gogcli`) also avoids the third-party-tap trust prompt the reporter hit.

Fixes #95017.

### Scope note

Only `gog`'s formula graduated to core. The other `steipete/tap/*` skills (`songsee`, `camsnap`, `peekaboo`, `imsg`, etc.) still resolve in that tap, so they are intentionally left unchanged. Verified `steipete/tap` still serves those formulae and no longer serves `gogcli`.

## Verification

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `gog` skill dependency install during onboarding fails because metadata references the removed `steipete/tap/gogcli` formula; switching to the homebrew-core `gogcli` formula makes `brew install gogcli` resolve.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux (WSL2) checkout. Dependency contract verified live against the Homebrew formulae API and the `steipete/homebrew-tap` repo; install-spec resolution exercised through the actual skill frontmatter parser + install-argv builder via Vitest.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # 1. Upstream dependency contract (live)
  curl -fsSL https://formulae.brew.sh/api/formula/gogcli.json | head -c 200
  curl -fsSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steipete/homebrew-tap/main/Formula/gogcli.rb

  # 2. Real parser -> install spec -> argv (disposable Vitest, since removed)
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/loading/frontmatter.gog-formula.proof.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `formulae.brew.sh/api/formula/gogcli.json` returns `"full_name":"gogcli","tap":"homebrew/core",...,"stable":"0.29.0"` (homepage `https://gogcli.sh`).
  - `steipete/homebrew-tap/.../Formula/gogcli.rb` returns HTTP `404` (formula removed); sibling formulae such as `songsee.rb` still return `200`.
  - The frontmatter parser resolves `gog`'s brew install spec to `formula: "gogcli"`, and the install-argv builder (`buildInstallArgv` in `src/skills/lifecycle/install.ts`) produces `["brew", "install", "gogcli"]`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Install command for `gog` is `brew install gogcli`, which resolves against homebrew-core (matching the reporter's working manual install) instead of the removed/untrusted tap formula.
- **What was not tested:** A full end-to-end `openclaw onboard` run on a fresh VPS performing the actual `brew install` (no clean VPS + Homebrew sandbox available here). The failure and fix are deterministic from the resolved `brew install <formula>` argument, which is verified against the live Homebrew formula contract.

### Notes

- Data/metadata-only change (one line). No new permanent test added — there is no per-skill-formula test harness, and asserting a single data literal is low value; the disposable parser proof above was removed after confirming the result.
- AI-assisted.
target.author: zengwen-dt
